pick.rationale: Arizona holds a commanding statistical advantage as the 3rd-ranked team nationally with a +37.56 adjusted efficiency margin that exceeds Purdue's +30.5 mark by over 7 points. While Purdue possesses the nation's elite offense ranked 1st/2nd nationally, their mediocre defense ranked 36th-100th faces a severe mismatch against Arizona's balanced attack featuring a top-10 offense. The Wildcats' elite 3rd-ranked defense provides the critical edge in this strength-on-strength matchup, and with star Koa Peat fully returned from injury, Arizona's championship-caliber two-way excellence offers a reliable path to the Final Four.

reasoningStep.title: Arizona vs Purdue Elite Eight Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Arizona's elite defense (3rd) and balanced two-way efficiency (+37.56 margin) overcome Purdue's elite offense (1st/2nd) and mediocre defense (36th-100th). The 5-spot ranking gap and 7-point efficiency margin advantage favor the Wildcats.
evidence: Arizona ranked 3rd nationally on both KenPom and Torvik with +37.56 efficiency margin
Purdue ranked 8th nationally with +30.5 efficiency margin
Arizona's defense ranked 3rd vs Purdue's offense ranked 1st/2nd - critical matchup edge
Purdue's defense ranked 36th (Torvik)/100th (KenPom) vulnerable to Arizona's top-10 offense
Koa Peat returned from injury and playing for Arizona per prior picks and web sources
Historical 1/2 Elite Eight matchups favor the 1-seed approximately 65-70% of the time
